Sonali Shah interviews honoured guests and participants(n)

For her own project, Sarah takes on an old typewriter(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: The Department for Work and Pensions is meant to help disabled people get back into work. In this programme, reporter Richard Butchins discovers that the DWP has lost more employment tribunals for disability discrimination than any other employer in Britain. He investigates why the DWP has paid its own employees nearly a million pounds of public money in both tribunal pay-outs and out-of-court settlements(n)

programme.title: MasterChef

They are challenged to serve up their signature dish, with the judges' favourite two winning a coveted MasterChef apron and a place in the next round. The remaining four must come up with a dish using three specific ingredients, after which two cooks are sent home - leaving the remaining four to cook two courses for former contestants who have gone on to work in the food industry(n)

Rainie's past comes back to haunt her when an ex-client arrives at Coker's, and Tiffany feels left out as Iqra and Keegan work on their loan application(n)

The tape of Fi's drunken night with Carrie and Richard is leaked on to the internet, with devastating repercussions for her divorce. But as Hannah tries to console her, she discovers this is not the last secret that is going to come to light. Richie employs manipulative tactics that could have dangerous repercussions, and Ruth organises a visit by the lawyers to their old law school to assist the students - but a trip down memory lane ends badly(n)

programme.desc: The presenters explore a one-bedroom flat in Whitefield, Greater Manchester, a bungalow in Tipton in Derbyshire and a two-bedroom flat in a Victorian building in London's New Cross(n)

programme.title: Dom Does America
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood samples life working on a cattle ranch in Austin, Texas, where he gets to grips with farm machinery and faces one of his biggest fears, horses(n)

programme.title: Saved on Camera
programme.desc: A young boy has to take control of the car when his mother has a seizure behind the wheel. Elsewhere, an off-duty fireman helps a woman who has fallen into a crater in Bali(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Nick Hall and Gary Pe help two teams scour an antiques fair at the National Botanic Garden of Wales, while host Charlie Ross shares his passion for cricket(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Valerie helps a cancer patient face up to some surprising news. Meanwhile, Bear is caught between Daniel and Zara(n)

programme.title: Five Bedrooms
programme.desc: A hurried housewarming causes squabbles, while a couple's marriage crumbles. Drama, starring Kat Stewart, Stephen Peacocke, Doris Younane, Katie Robertson and Roy Joseph(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: City-dwelling families, couples and individuals set out to find their ideal rural retreats with the help of a property expert(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades salvages three items from a recycling centre in Altrincham, Greater Manchester, including a collection of slate tiles and a trio of metal lights(n)

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Friends Ola and Rhesa from Leicester compete in the culinary contest, teaming up with chefs Romy Gill and Anna Haugh. Rylan Clark-Neal hosts(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: Party Political Broadcast
programme.desc: By the Liberal Democrats(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio. Presented by Alex Jones and Matt Baker(n)

programme.title: The Heat Is On: Sport Relief
programme.desc: Nick Grimshaw, Frankie Bridge, Rob Rinder, Krishnan Guru-Murthy, Karim Zeroual, Samantha Womack and Louise Minchin attempt to cross the world's oldest desert in Namibia. In four days, the group must travel over 100 miles, scale the highest sand dunes on Earth and avoid deadly wildlife, all under the glare of the blistering desert sun, to finally reach the world-famous Eduard Bohlen shipwreck on Africa's skeleton coast(n)

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: Another group of six amateur cooks prepare a Signature Dish for judges John Torode and Gregg Wallace, hoping to secure their place in the next stage of the competition. After two contestants are sent home, the remaining four take part in The Three Ingredients Challenge, creating a dish showcasing lamb, aubergine and red pepper(n)

programme.title: Eating with My Ex
programme.desc: Ryan will stop at nothing to win Faye from York back. Nick wants answers from his ex, Tiff. After the high school sweethearts separated for uni, he needs to know if she cheated on him - but she has her own questions for him, too. Tion tries to convince Aaliyah that they still have a future together. Narrated by Alice Levine(n)

programme.title: Eating with My Ex
programme.desc: Shell tries to prove to Laura that she is sorry for cheating on her, and Winston wants to know why Shannon ended their two-year relationship and if they warrant a second chance. Narrated by Alice Levine(n)

programme.title: Killing Eve
programme.desc: Having survived a terrifying close call with Villanelle, Eve and the team now have the mole retrieved. In a safe house, they question their prisoner, hoping to uncover information that could lead them not only to Villanelle, but also to whoever is calling the shots behind her. However, the assassin is still in the country, and none of the team are safe while she is still at large. Spy thriller, starring Jodie Comer, Sandra Oh, Fiona Shaw, Kim Bodnia and Darren Boyd(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore turns to upcycler Jacqui Joseph to transform a pair of chairs and a laundry basket, while designer-maker Daniel Heath hopes to give a small melamine dining table a stylish update. A pair of French armoires give Sarah herself an exciting new challenge(n)

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Sinead's love of cooking came when grandmother Jean taught her as a little girl. However, as she's travelled around the world her tastes have got more adventurous. Cooking against the clock, they are paired up with chefs Anna Haugh and Mike Reid and produce dishes from around the world on a budget of £5, from squid tempura to pad Thai and Korean chicken wings. Plus, the 10-minute challenge throws up cornflakes and ginger nut biscuits. Hosted by Rylan Clarke-Neal(n)

programme.title: Best Home Cook
programme.desc: The contestants are given just three hours to produce their ultimate children's birthday cake - and as if that were not tough enough, Mary Berry reminds them she knows a thing or two about baking. This is followed by Chris Bavin's challenge to rustle something up from leftovers, and he makes the task twice as hard by stripping back the contents of the larders. The judges then decide who will have to compete in Angela Hartnett's eliminator, by the end of which another contestant will be hanging up their apron. Claudia Winkleman hosts(n)

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: BBC Parliament's programme looking back at the week in Westminster presented by Mandy Baker.(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Jo Coburn and guests discuss the big political issues of the day(n)

programme.title: Women's Six Nations Highlights
programme.desc: Action from the fourth round of fixtures, including England v Wales at Twickenham Stoop and Scotland v France at Scotstoun Stadium(n)

programme.title: Best House in Town
programme.desc: Three detached properties in Leicestershire are in the spotlight, featuring a retro family home, a country-style new-build and a house with some seriously modern twists(n)

programme.title: Royal Recipes
programme.desc: Michael Buerk celebrates royal food inspired by the days of India and Empire, as Paul Ainsworth cooks up quail and potato curry, said to be a favourite of Queen Victoria. Plus, historian Dr Annie Gray visits Osborne House on the Isle of Wight where Victoria, Empress of India, showcased her grand passion for the raj, and even hired her own Indian chefs(n)

programme.title: Terry and Mason's Great Food Trip
programme.desc: Broadcaster Terry Wogan and London cabbie Mason McQueen continue their culinary journey around the nation in Scarborough, North Yorkshire. In their quest to find the best local food, they visit a fish and chip cafe with a glamorous history, go on a train ride and attend a tea-dance with more than 30 different varieties of cake for them to sample(n)

programme.title: Red Rock
programme.desc: Keith faces a watery demise while trying to buy Mick's silence, Paudge is reluctant to break good news, and Sean may have an ulterior motive in talking to Irena(n)

programme.title: The Great Interior Design Challenge
programme.desc: The two remaining designers compete for a place in the quarter final, this time transforming two tiny bedrooms in medieval timber-framed houses in Sussex. The pair have very different briefs, with one designer facing the challenge of creating a Scandinavian-inspired scheme with a raised built-in bed. The other must deliver a Batman-inspired fantasy den, complete with bat motif lighting, a secret compartment, and dark grey walls. Presented by Tom Dyckhoff(n)

programme.title: Portillo's State Secrets
programme.desc: Michael Portillo looks at files from the National Archives that relate to traitors and spies, including documents that show a king's role in torture and James Bond's secret wartime origins. He also learns how a double agent's escape at the height of the Cold War was a comedy of errors(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Experts James Lewis and Jonathan Pratt assess items brought to Dover Castle in Kent, while presenter Paul Martin looks at the life of James Bond creator Ian Fleming(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Previous winners Hugh Dennis, Sarah Greene, Nish Kumar and Holly Walsh go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo sets off on the first leg of a journey from the Midlands to Rye in East Sussex, beginning by travelling from Warwick to Radley in Oxfordshire(n)

programme.title: Coastal Path
programme.desc: Paul Rose explores the spectacular coastline of southern Cornwall, discovering one of the world's finest stages at the Minack open-air theatre in Porthcurno. He also tries out the high-octane sport of coasteering on the Lizard Peninsula, joins a National Trust ranger for some clifftop wildlife spotting, and fires the Tudor cannons of Pendennis Castle in Falmouth(n)

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys invites four more contenders to brave the black chair, answering questions on the specialist subjects of the life and written works of Laura Ingalls Wilder, Star Trek: The Original Series, the life and career of Jenson Button, and Mary, Queen of Scots. They then each have two minutes to demonstrate their general knowledge(n)

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell presents the first semi-final of the quiz as two teams compete to guess the connections between things that seem utterly random at first glance, with one set of clues comprising Numbers, Thunderball, Cuthbert, and Sailor(n)

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The quarter-final matches continue as two more teams of four students compete to make it to the next stage of the competition as they try to win two matches and progress to the semi-finals. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions(n)

programme.title: Miriam's Big Fat Adventure
programme.desc: Part one of two. Actress Miriam Margolyes travels around the nation to discover why so many people are overweight, despite so much information about healthy lifestyles. She wants to understand what impact being obese has on people and how the rest of society views them and begins by meeting people fighting back against their size - both physically and psychologically(n)

programme.title: Inside No 9
programme.desc: Two police officers always have time to get to know each other while working on the night shift together. However, PC Thompson's previous partner died in a brutal attack less than a month previously, the forensic evidence has gone missing and PCSO Varney is beginning to ask a lot of awkward questions. The final instalment of the fifth series of the anthology of darkly comic tales, written by and starring The League of Gentlemen and Psychoville's Steve Pemberton and Reece Shearsmith, with Rebecca Callard and Malik Ibheis(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's important national and international news stories, presented by Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: The documentary focuses on the work of staff at Alder Hey Children's Hospital. Eight-month-old Elsie was not expected to survive being born due to serious kidney abnormalities. But she has spent all but two weeks of her life in hospital and has 11 operations to repair the damage caused by a genetic problem. Now she faces surgery yet again. Holly has cerebral palsy and, as she is about to turn 18, is preparing to transition to adult services in Liverpool - a huge leap for her parents who have grown to depend on the hospital's care(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: International sheepdog breeder David and farmer Brenda battle the temperamental British weather with their pedigree dogs by their sides, competing in Powburn, Northumberland, at one of Britain's oldest sheepdog trials(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team visits the Gwent Levels to find out more about a project celebrating the area's fishing and farming heritage, and preserving them for the future. Matt Baker meets a group of fishermen who are still catching salmon the way it's been done for centuries, Ellie Harrison is on the trail of a star species that's made an amazing comeback to the Levels, and Tom Heap is looking at what's being done to ensure those most in need in the countryside can access the healthiest fresh food(n)

programme.title: Secrets of the Museum
programme.desc: Behind the scenes at the V&A, curator Keith is trying to breathe life into an original Stormtrooper costume from The Empire Strikes Back. However, the helmet is missing. In the Rock and Pop archive, curator Vicky is examining a photograph donated after the V&A's David Bowie Exhibition. And a road sign, made in 1961 by graphic designer Margaret Calvert, is also added to the museum's collection(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning and Christina Trevanion present from the Shropshire town of Oswestry, where two teams shop for antiques with the help of experts Kate Bliss and Ochuko Ojiri. Anita also meets Per Lindstrand, a pioneer who made history with his balloon flights(n)

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Friends and former scout leaders Anne and Jenny take part in the culinary contest, teaming up with chefs Romy Gill and Akis Petretzikis. Rylan Clark-Neal hosts(n)

programme.title: Spy in the Wild
programme.desc: Natural history documentary in which spy cameras camouflaged as various creatures go 'undercover' in the animal world, getting up close and personal to film their behaviour. On Christmas Island, the Spy Crab camera walks among red crabs as they march into the sea, and in Madagascar, cameras follow many of the species of chameleon that live there. Meanwhile on Seal island off the tip of South Africa, Spy Seal Pup finds itself in the midst of the 60,000 cape fur seals that have come ashore to raise their young(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond(n)

programme.title: The Super League Show
programme.desc: Tanya Arnold introduces action from the latest fixtures, including Huddersfield v St Helens, Catalans Dragons v Salford Red Devils and Wakefield Trinity v Hull FC(n)

programme.title: Best House in Town
programme.desc: The owners of a traditional Edwardian end-of-terrace, a Victorian terrace brought crashing into the 21st century, and a terrace that has been stretched to its limit all compete(n)

programme.title: Terry and Mason's Great Food Trip
programme.desc: Terry Wogan and Mason McQueen's motoring tour takes them to the ancient city of Chester. Their first stop is a cheese shop, where they taste the county's famous Cheshire cheese before heading off to a new type of farmer's market to try local producer's specialities. They also eat baked potatoes in roman ruins, a Chester tart next to the city walls, pasta in the Cathedral and are served a Civil War feast at one of the town's oldest pubs(n)

programme.title: Red Rock
programme.desc: Keith's fate is revealed, and Aoife's statement proves unexpectedly damaging. Irish crime drama, starring Patrick Ryan and Stephen Cromwell(n)

programme.title: The Great Interior Design Challenge
programme.desc: Four more amateur designers take up the challenge as they each attempt to transform a cottage in the Peak District village of Tissington, with three days, £1,000 and a small team of assistants to help deliver their projects. Tom Dyckhoff examines the history of the community, before judges Sophie Robinson and Daniel Hopwood decide which contender is to leave the competition(n)

programme.title: Portillo's State Secrets
programme.desc: Michael Portillo looks at files from the National Archives that relate to the monarchy, unearthing a speech that would have been read by the Queen in the event of the Third World War breaking out. He also examines the fabulous wealth of a tragic king and reveals the nine lives of Queen Victoria(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Presenter Paul Martin and experts Natasha Raskin Sharp and James Lewis visit Bristol Cathedral, where Natasha is intrigued by an unusual walking stick with a top made from a German Zeppelin, and James comes across a collection of lead soldiers. Paul learns about the origins of Bristol's vibrant street art scene and has a go at making a graffiti stencil.(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Former winners Hugh Dennis, Sarah Greene, Nish Kumar and Holly Walsh go head to head testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo picks up his rail journey from Warwick to Rye. In Reading, he traces the origins of education for workers, and in Cliveden explores a great Italianate mansion(n)

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: Five pairs of competitors journey around the globe, carrying only limited cash and unable to get on a plane or use mobile phones and credit cards. In the first edition, the teams set off from Mexico City, hoping to reach the first of seven checkpoints - Copan Ruinas in Honduras. Each team adopts a different strategy, with married couple Jen and Rob quickly deciding on a route via Belize, but confusion on the Metro leads to a delay(n)

programme.title: Back in Time for...
programme.desc: Billy Bragg pays the Arderns a visit as they get to grips with 1960s life. The new decade brings a dizzying number of new developments, including skiffle music, hula hoops and sliced bread. It also brought about a seismic change in shopping with the introduction of self-service. The rise of supermarkets sees the personal service of the Arderns' shop under threat, forcing them to diversify their stock. The 1960s saw Sheffield becoming home to new workers from across the Commonwealth, many of whom share their memories of the era and the importance of the local corner shop to shift workers(n)

programme.title: Miriam's Big Fat Adventure
programme.desc: Part two of two. Miriam Margolyes sets out to find out what is being done to combat the obesity crisis, and meets people who are going to extreme and sometimes controversial lengths to lose weight. To understand the true cost of the epidemic, Miriam meets 41-year-old Angela, whose leg was amputated two years ago. Her weight meant she needed a knee operation, which led to a sepsis infection. Stuck at home relying on carers to wash, dress and feed her, Miriam is shocked to learn how Angela's weight upended her life(n)

programme.title: Better Things
programme.desc: Sam has some girl time with friends, but is less than happy when a husband returns home early. At home, Duke is concerned about the presence of her late grandfather in her bedroom(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events. Presented by Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Aldo Kane reports on the illegal tiger trade in South East Asia, with the endangered big cats trafficked for products including wine and glue made from their bones. He works alongside wildlife crime investigators working the key trafficking routes in Malaysia, China, Thailand, Laos and Vietnam, and uncovers shocking footage of breeding facilities and animals being fattened for slaughter. With fewer than 4,000 tigers left in the wild, this profitable illegal enterprise threatens to wipe the species out altogether(n)

programme.title: Win the Wilderness: Alaska
programme.desc: Duane and Rena question each couple about their plans for the home to see if any of them are harbouring doubts about their ability to make a success of life in the wilderness. This round of questioning results in one couple being eliminated from the contest, while the remaining three are separated from each other for two days to test their ability to cope with isolation(n)

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein revisit 1926 to explore a case of Leeds landlady Lily Waterhouse who died, it was believed, at the hands of her lodger Louie Calvert, who had only been living at the house for a matter of weeks(n)

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: There are serious concerns for a baby boy who arrives in A&E and the team members need to make a quick decision about whether they can help him, or whether he requires specialist care 225 miles away on mainland Scotland. Meanwhile, occupational therapist Marc Beswick returns to long-term patient Summer. The four-year-old has a condition that means it's hard for her to move her arms and legs. Marc has been working on her electric wheelchair, making modifications to make sure she can develop some independence(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore and the team are at Wallyford Recycling Centre in East Lothian, where they restore a broken games table, a pair of old lamps and a tired display cabinet(n)

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Anna Haugh and Akis Petretzikis create a meal in 20 minutes, using ingredients brought in by a couple who have developed a renewed interest in healthy eating. With Rylan Clark-Neal(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo crosses the county line from Devon into Somerset, seeing first-hand how willow farmers sought to overcome the challenge from the production of synthetic plastics during the 1930s and hearing how tourism has spread across the region, especially amongst the young. In Bath, Michael visits the former home of a refugee emperor, whose country was invaded by the Italian dictator Mussolini, and in whose name a religious movement began that now flourishes worldwide. Travelling east into Wiltshire, he reaches the largest training area of the British Army, Salisbury Plain(n)

programme.title: Yorkshire Walks
programme.desc: Artist Shanaz Gulzar walks through the Bolton Abbey Estate, heading out from Bolton Priory along the River Wharfe and over moorland to her journey's end at Simon's Seat and its views across Wharfedale, Nidderdale and beyond. Meeting fellow walkers along the way, Shanaz finds inspirational spots to sketch and recite poetry, while discovering hidden woodlands and waterfalls(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Andrew Neil presents live coverage of Chancellor Rishi Sunak's 2020 Budget with expert analysis from Laura Kuenssberg, Faisal Islam and Simon Jack. Includes Prime Minister's Questions at noon(n)

programme.title: Red Rock
programme.desc: Dunne needs to damage Aoife's credibility, Patricia may need to stomach an illicit solution for her faulty flats and Bridget is bothered in her own home(n)

programme.title: The Great Interior Design Challenge
programme.desc: In the second round of this heat, the three remaining designers take on one of the most demanding rooms to redesign on a tight budget - the kitchen. They're each working in a Victorian Half-House, in Walthamstow, North East London, where architectural historian Tom Dyckhoff looks at the story behind these early apartments, and the family who built them(n)

programme.title: Portillo's State Secrets
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his search through the National Archives, unearthing files outlining the state's strategy on Scientology, exploring how bull running was outlawed in Britain, and taking a look at pictures by John Lennon that were banned(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At Huddersfield Town Hall, West Yorkshire, Nick Davies is drawn to a bobbin holder that reveals the town's textile heritage, while Christina Trevanion finds an incredible collection of glass scent bottles. Presenter, Paul Martin delves into the area's coal mining history(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Former winners Hugh Dennis, Sarah Greene, Nish Kumar and Holly Walsh test their knowledge in a series of trivia-based games across the week(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo reaches the capital on his rail journey from Warwick to Rye in East Sussex. In Ealing, he is transformed for the silver screen by expert hair and make-up, before travelling to Fulham, where he heads underground to explore London's new super sewer. Then, Michael visits the London School of Economics and Political Science in central London, before finally arriving in Kensington(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Neil Show
programme.desc: Interviews, discussion and analysis from Westminster, including the latest on Brexit with Andrew Neil and guests(n)

programme.title: Grand Tours of Scotland's Lochs
programme.desc: Paul Murton travels around the firths of Easter Ross and Sutherland, beginning at Loch Ussie - famous for its connections with the Brahan Seer, who predicted future calamities. On a golf course above Strathpeffer, he discovers an unlikely connection between the conventional old spa town below and occultist and author Aleister Crowley, before heading for the Dornoch Firth, where he encounters a witch and a fairy bridge(n)

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: A crew of RNLI volunteers in Newquay is called out to a shipwreck off the Cornish coast, where a diver who has lost his mouthpiece needs rescuing from 30 metres underwater. A yacht in the Dover Strait starts to sink - and may collide with a cargo ship before going under - and a Dorset crew search for a windsurfer who has got caught in a storm(n)

programme.title: Child of Our Time
programme.desc: In 1999, the groundbreaking BBC series Child of Our Time began filming a group of babies from the moment they were born, to explore what would shape their lives in the new millennium. Twenty years on ,this programme catches us with Eve, Jamie and Rhianna from the project, with the three reflecting on growing up during a time of extraordinary social change(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Sitcom star and The Last Leg regular Josh Widdicombe plays host in this show recorded before a packed house at London's Hammersmith Apollo. He'll be warming up the audience and then introducing routines by Londoner Nathan Caton and Celia Pacquola from Australia(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events. Presented by Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Entrepreneurs pitch their money-making ideas to a panel of self-made millionaires, who are prepared to invest their company's money in the scheme if they think it could work(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Francesco's Mediterranean Voyage
programme.desc: Francesco da Mosto follows the modern-day pilgrimage route to the island of Tinos and its reputedly miraculous statue of the Virgin Mary as he sails through the Cyclades archipelago. On Delos, he investigates the temples of a more ancient religion, while Naxos is the site for an octopus fishing expedition(n)

programme.title: Dynasties
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a documentary following five of the world's most endangered species as they struggle to preserve their social groups, beginning with a chimpanzee troop in Senegal. As the dry season sets in, the Alpha of the pack, who has ruled unchallenged for the past three years, faces a series of challenges and finds little support from the other apes(n)

programme.title: Age of the Image
programme.desc: Art historian James Fox explores how mass communication and new technology helped 20th-century image-makers transform society, as films, photographs, TV, art and advertising all became weapons in the ideological battles of the age. He reveals how actress Marlene Dietrich and director Leni Riefenstahl each used cinema to pursue very different visions of power and freedom, and how Jewish comic book artists in New York created superheroes as their act of resistance to the Nazi threat(n)

programme.title: Suffragettes with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: To mark 100 years since women over the age of 30, who owned property, were first allowed to vote in the UK, Lucy Worsley examines the battle that women had to wage for that right. She tells the story a group of less well-known young working-class suffragettes who decided to go against every rule and expectation that Edwardian society had about them, and ended up being tracked by a special police unit(n)

programme.title: Art of France
programme.desc: Andrew Graham-Dixon examines the development of Impressionism, when France was changing at a rapid pace and young artists re-invented the rules of painting - finding their muses in the bars, brothels and cabarets of Paris and turning the world of art on its head. Monet, Degas and their contemporaries launched a heated debate about the role of painting in the modern world that would pave the way for just about every modern art movement of note(n)

programme.title: Britain's Outlaws: Highwaymen, Pirates and Rogues
programme.desc: Sam Willis concludes his exploration of the criminal underworld of the 17th and 18th centuries by looking at urban crime, fraud and corruption, uncovering a fascinating rogues' gallery of villains. He examines the case of thief and serial escaper Jack Sheppard, who was so notorious that almost a quarter of a million people turned up to witness his hanging, while fraudster Mary Toft managed to convince George I's surgeon that she had given birth to rabbits(n)

programme.title: Rude Britannia
programme.desc: A look at lewd British humour from the 1960s to the present, examining the tradition of cartooning, with Gerald Scarfe, Steve Bell and Martin Rowson providing examples of their work. The programme also examines the rude comic art of Viz magazine, the innuendo-laden radio show Round the Horne, the satirical underground magazine Oz and the dark humour of playwright Joe Orton(n)

programme.title: Age of the Image
programme.desc: Art historian James Fox explores how mass communication and new technology helped 20th-century image-makers transform society, as films, photographs, TV, art and advertising all became weapons in the ideological battles of the age. He reveals how actress Marlene Dietrich and director Leni Riefenstahl each used cinema to pursue very different visions of power and freedom, and how Jewish comic book artists in New York created superheroes as their act of resistance to the Nazi threat(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Gareth Edwards' Great Welsh Adventure
programme.desc: Though aged in his 70s, the rugby legend is busier than ever, so wife Maureen persuades him to take life a little easier by exploring the four Welsh canals. The duo take charge of a narrowboat on the Llangollen Canal, but Gareth's stress levels go soon through the roof as he tries to avoid hitting obstacles. Somehow, Wales' much-loved couple still find time to appreciate the breath-taking scenery(n)

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.desc: The history of the steamers which were once a common sight on rivers and coastal waters around Britain and were a highlight of seaside holidays from the 1820s until the early 1960s, providing the adventure and trappings of an ocean voyage without venturing out of sight of land(n)

programme.title: Ian Hislop's Olden Days
programme.desc: The satirist explores the nation's obsession with the past to reveal how and why the British have continually plundered the 'olden days' to make sense of and shape the present. He begins by examining the various spins put on the lives of King Arthur and Alfred the Great through the ages. He finds out how Arthur has been variously depicted as a wild Celtic warlord, a chivalric hero and a piously questing king, while Alfred's character was manipulated to suit the purposes of tricky medieval lawyers, a Tudor archbishop and a Georgian Prince of Wales(n)

programme.title: Lost Sitcoms
programme.desc: A remake of the episode A Woman's Place is in the Home, originally broadcast in 1967. Alf arrives home to find a burnt supper is the least of his worries. Simon Day stars(n)

programme.title: Armada - 12 Days to Save England
programme.desc: Dan Snow concludes the three-part drama-documentary exploring the battle against the Spanish Armada. The final episode reveals the circumstances that led to the defeat of the invasion force, drawing on recently rediscovered documents, and also explores how victory enhanced Elizabeth I's reputation as a world leader - while many of the sailors who fought in the battle died of starvation and disease. Featuring dramatic reconstructions, starring Anita Dobson as Elizabeth(n)

programme.title: Inside the Medieval Mind
programme.desc: Professor Robert Bartlett reveals the framework of the medieval class system. He explores why family status determined the rights of an individual and reveals the truth behind the myth of the chivalrous knight. He goes on to explain the positive role the Black Death played in bringing about the change in the social order(n)

programme.title: A Victorian Scandal: The Rudest Book in Britain
programme.desc: Historian Dr Fern Riddell uncovers the real truth about Victorian attitudes to sex, featuring an in depth look at a scandalous Victorian sex trial that took place in 1877(n)

programme.title: Sex, Chips and Poetry: 50 Years of The Mersey Sound
programme.desc: Fifty years ago, Penguin published poetry anthology The Mersey Sound, introducing Roger McGough, Brian Patten and Adrian Henri to a worldwide audience. In this documentary, McGough and Patten are joined by famous friends and fellow writers to tell the inside story of this modern classic and how they made poetry cool(n)

programme.title: Genius of the Ancient World
programme.desc: Bettany Hughes' series exploring the lives and teachings of influential ancient philosophers draws to a close with a profile of Confucius, who is credited as the first Chinese thinker to take a systematic philosophical approach to issues of social, political and ethical importance. Born during the chaotic Spring and Autumn period of Chinese history, Confucius believed that harmony could be promoted and achieved through the examples of sage rulers. He was also a innovative teacher, and tried to instill his principles of moral excellence in China's elite, but died without seeing his vision for his nation come to pass. However, his philosophies would eventually form the bedrock of Chinese culture, with many of his principles of tradition surviving to this day(n)

programme.title: Ian Hislop's Olden Days
programme.desc: The satirist explores the nation's obsession with the past to reveal how and why the British have continually plundered the 'olden days' to make sense of and shape the present. He begins by examining the various spins put on the lives of King Arthur and Alfred the Great through the ages. He finds out how Arthur has been variously depicted as a wild Celtic warlord, a chivalric hero and a piously questing king, while Alfred's character was manipulated to suit the purposes of tricky medieval lawyers, a Tudor archbishop and a Georgian Prince of Wales(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Handmade on the Silk Road
programme.desc: Documentary series focusing on the traditional crafts along the ancient trade route of the Silk Road, capturing the techniques used by master craftspeople at work. The first edition is set in North West China, where the Uyghur community has been making Atlas silk for thousands of years. It follows a weaver, Mattursun Islam, and his family as they continue the tradition and produce silk in over 200 designs in a small workshop(n)

programme.title: The Victorian House of Arts and Crafts
programme.desc: Using original tools and techniques, the six 21st-century crafters are set to make from scratch a Victorian double bed and bed spread, a bedside clock and plaster-wall decoration in a week, all the while eating, working and living within the philosophies first outlined by the likes of John Ruskin and William Morris. Will their 1890s communal life help them to better understand the depth and scale of the arts and crafts movement, both as a power for artistic and social change? Once again, host Anita Rani is joined by contributors Keith Brymer Jones and Patch Rogers(n)

programme.title: Live Women's International Football
programme.desc: England Women v Spain Women (Kick-off 9.15pm). Eilidh Barbour presents coverage of both teams' concluding match of the round-robin competition, held at Toyota Stadium in Frisco, Texas. England prevailed 2-1 when the sides last met ahead of the 2019 World Cup courtesy of goals from Beth Mead and Ellen White, and another closely contested match is expected tonight(n)

Toodloo helps the line get a little longer still but when Great BigHoo taps the screen, the line becomes so long it shoots off the Twirlywoo Screen. programme.desc: Anita Manning and Christina Trevanion present from the Shropshire town of Oswestry, where two teams shop for antiques with the help of experts Kate Bliss and Ochuko Ojiri. Anita also meets Per Lindstrand, a pioneer who made history with his balloon flights(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Daniel and Zara have fun trying to keep a secret, while a determined Bear returns from his break. Al tries to help the owner of his favourite pub, whose life is falling apart(n)

programme.title: A Service of Celebration for Commonwealth Day
programme.desc: In the presence of the Queen and other senior members of the royal family, Petroc Trelawny presents live coverage from Westminster Abbey of a service featuring readings, music and dance. Including performances by Alexandra Burke, Craig David and Melodians Steel Orchestra, as well as a reflection by world heavyweight boxing champion Anthony Joshua. Sonali Shah interviews honoured guests and participants(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: At Witley Recycling Centre, upcycler Sarah Moore is on the lookout for three unloved items she can save from the skip, salvage and sell on for a profit. To help transform her trio of tip-finds Sarah turns to furniture re-styler Emma Walker to help overhaul a pair of seats, while upholsterer Anthony Devine is challenged with bringing an Edwardian armchair into the 21st Century. For her own project, Sarah takes on an old typewriter(n)

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Friends and former scout leaders Anne and Jenny take part in the culinary contest, teaming up with chefs Romy Gill and Akis Petretzikis. Rylan Clark-Neal hosts(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Matt Baker present the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat(n)

programme.title: The Chronicles of Erne
programme.desc: The programme documents autumn on the lough, with Frankie and Eddie Roofe taking part in the annual Erne Pike Classic fishing competition and Joe Kelly juggling running an artisan bakery with spending as much time as possible on his parents' island(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Chantelle comforts Gray when his office returns his belongings although he cannot face telling Whitney what has happened. Jean accuses Suki of lying about her illness, leaving her family disgusted that she could suggest such a thing. Linda is hurt that Mick doesn't trust her when she finds him searching for stashed alcohol. Jay makes a big decision about his future(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: The Department for Work and Pensions is meant to help disabled people get back into work. In this programme, reporter Richard Butchins discovers that the DWP has lost more employment tribunals for disability discrimination than any other employer in Britain. He investigates why the DWP has paid its own employees nearly a million pounds of public money in both tribunal pay-outs and out-of-court settlements(n)

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: John Torode and Gregg Wallace preside as six more hopefuls compete in the third week of heats. They are challenged to serve up their signature dish, with the judges' favourite two winning a coveted MasterChef apron and a place in the next round. The remaining four must come up with a dish using three specific ingredients, after which two cooks are sent home - leaving the remaining four to cook two courses for former contestants who have gone on to work in the food industry(n)

programme.title: There's No Place Like Tyrone
programme.desc: Lynda challenges her partner Bobby to spice up date night, while Ruth and her dad John go shopping for a new beauty salon, albeit in very different places(n)

programme.title: This Country
programme.desc: Kurtan receives some sad news about a relative and endeavours to make some new memories. Meanwhile, Kerry discovers a passion for reading and joins the vicar's book club, which goes well until another village resident joins. Spoof documentary, starring Daisy May and Charlie Cooper(n)

programme.title: Young, Welsh and Pretty Minted
programme.desc: The programme meets 20-year-old Callum Griffiths, who is one of Europe's up-and-coming suppliers of dog biscuits, and designer Ibby Tarafdar, who spends half of his time jet-setting to meet his famous American clients, and the other half working as a barista. Instagram star Erin Budina is branching out with a YouTube channel that challenges her to do everyday jobs - but will working in a recycling depot prove a step too far for her?(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Eddie Izzard hosts an evening of stand-up at the Apollo in Hammersmith, London, treating the audience to his own brand of stream-of-consciousness humour before introducing routines by The Last Leg star Josh Widdicombe and South African comic Trevor Noah(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley hears from the rural policing teams in North Yorkshire who are working to tackle the persecution of birds of prey, and Rav Wilding presents the latest appeals(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Martel Maxwell and Dion Dublin follow the development progress of properties in Pimlico, London, Ilkeston, Derbyshire and Chesterton, near Newcastle-under-Lyme(n)

programme.title: Dom Does America
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood takes on his toughest challenge yet, working as a paramedic in Austin, Texas. His mentor is Cassi Lydon, a veteran paramedic with 14 years' service(n)

programme.title: Saved on Camera
programme.desc: A man fights off masked house raiders to save his wife, a manta ray faces certain death if a fish hook is not removed, and in America, drivers stop to save a woman in a flash flood(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Ben Cooper and Charles Hanson help contestants in Ballinderry, Co Antrim, look for items to sell at auction, while Anita Manning visits the Crown Bar pub in Belfast(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Al and Emma search for a breakthrough in Jimmi's case, Zara and Daniel try to keep their emotions in check, and Ruhma encounters a couple who are not what they appear to be(n)

programme.title: Five Bedrooms
programme.desc: Drama about five loosely connected friends at the singles table at a wedding who, after too many champagnes, wines, whiskeys and beers, decide to pool together to get on the property ladder. There's just one glaring problem - they'll have to live with each other. Starring Kat Stewart, Stephen Peacocke, Doris Younane, Katie Robertson, Roy Joseph, Kate Jenkinson and Hugh Sheridan(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps a couple find a new home in South Yorkshire with room enough for their children and any future grandchildren. Plus, a dog-sledding race through the woods(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore and the team are at Wallyford Recycling Centre in East Lothian, where they restore a broken games table, a pair of old lamps and a tired display cabinet(n)

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Anna Haugh and Akis Petretzikis create a meal in 20 minutes, using ingredients brought in by a couple who have developed a renewed interest in healthy eating. With Rylan Clark-Neal(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Matt Baker present another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: After practising his proposal technique, Jay organises a romantic lunch and pops the question to Lola. Will she agree to make an honest man of him? Gray reveals he is planning to represent Whitney in an attempt to win back his position at the law firm, while Chantelle declines Kheerat's offer of a job - but soon has reason to regret it. Rainie's past comes back to haunt her when an ex-client arrives at Coker's, and Tiffany feels left out as Iqra and Keegan work on their loan application(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Cameron sets out to make a good impression during a hectic day at work, unaware that a suspicious Xavier has unearthed a shocking secret that could ruin him. Sacha, Essie, Dom and Ben are still reeling from the recent revelations when they are forced to work together to save the life of a vulnerable child. Fletch fears that a combination of budget cuts, staff shortages, and Max's inflexible attitude will spell disaster for the hospital, but it is he who is really at breaking point(n)

programme.title: The Split
programme.desc: Hannah's affair is now out in the open, and she desperately tries to make amends to the people she has deceived and save her marriage. The tape of Fi's drunken night with Carrie and Richard is leaked on to the internet, with devastating repercussions for her divorce. But as Hannah tries to console her, she discovers this is not the last secret that is going to come to light. Richie employs manipulative tactics that could have dangerous repercussions, and Ruth organises a visit by the lawyers to their old law school to assist the students - but a trip down memory lane ends badly(n)

programme.title: Spotlight
programme.desc: Mandy McAuley investigates why Northern Ireland has the highest suicide rate in the UK and speaks to those on the frontline determined to reduce it(n)

programme.title: Beauty Laid Bare
programme.desc: The group of four question the true cost of the beauty industry. On their quest for answers they meet a wide range of people, from farmers in Mexico supplying to industry, to the homeless community in Skid Row and the LAPD(n)

programme.title: War on Plastic with Hugh and Anita
programme.desc: Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall and Anita Rani explore the gigantic problem of plastic waste pollution, finding out where it is coming from and what can be done to try to solve the issue. Working with residents of a street in Bristol, Anita quickly identifies supermarkets as the main source of single-use plastics, while Hugh is on the trail of the ubiquitous plastic water bottle(n)

programme.desc: A young boy has to take control of the car when his mother has a seizure behind the wheel. Elsewhere, an off-duty fireman helps a woman who has fallen into a crater in Bali(n)

programme.title: The Heat Is On: Sport Relief
programme.desc: Nick Grimshaw, Frankie Bridge, Rob Rinder, Krishnan Guru-Murthy, Karim Zeroual, Samantha Womack and Louise Minchin attempt to cross the world's oldest desert in Namibia. In four days, the group must travel over 100 miles, scale the highest sand dunes on Earth and avoid deadly wildlife, all under the glare of the blistering desert sun, to finally reach the world-famous Eduard Bohlen shipwreck on Africa's skeleton coast(n)

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: Another group of six amateur cooks prepare a Signature Dish for judges John Torode and Gregg Wallace, hoping to secure their place in the next stage of the competition. programme.desc: Dugie restores a piece of dinosaur bone, the Strictly Skye dance event is held in Portree, and Duntulm crofters take their sheep home from Trodday island(n)

programme.title: 'San Fhuil (It's in the Blood)
programme.desc: A personal portrait of a South Uist crofting township where the Greylag geese outnumber people by three to one. Over the last 30 years, the resident population of Greylags has increased from less than 1,000 to over 9,000 birds. With little to check the breeding Greylag, the geese are destroying the cultivation of Uist corn(n)

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot(n)

programme.title: The Chase

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Gary asks Sharon to teach Ali a lesson, and a reporter asks James about rumours regarding his sexuality circulating on social media. Roy's fellow bat-watchers are horrified by Nina's attire and deeply suspicious of her motives(n)

programme.title: St Davids - Britain's Smallest City
programme.desc: The summer holidays bring thousands of tourists to St Davids, causing traffic chaos on the high street, while the boat trips search for dolphins, and a water shortage could spell disaster for some of the residents of Britain's smallest city(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: A terrified Gary leaves a message for Sharon telling her that if Ali dies, she is to blame, and James receives a worrying text from his manager. Roy resigns from the bat-watching group after some of the members' remarks about Nina's dress sense(n)

programme.title: Liar
programme.desc: Feeling the full force of DI Renton's investigation, Laura looks to those closest to her for support, but when suspicions fall on Ian, she is left questioning who she can trust. Seeking answers, she turns to a former ally, but the information she gleans only fuels her concerns. Meanwhile in the past, Oliver brings Andrew some devastating news, leading him to make a reckless and dangerous excursion. Jack and Harry Williams's thriller, starring Joanne Froggatt and Ioan Gruffudd, with Katherine Kelly and Sam Spruell(n)

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents(n)

programme.title: Serial Killer with Piers Morgan
programme.desc: An interview with Lorenzo Gilyard, who was convicted of killing six people in and around Missouri between 1977 and 1993, and suspected of murdering a further seven more, earning him six life sentences. His prolific spree saw him labelled the Kansas City Strangler, and despite overwhelming DNA evidence pointing to his guilt, he continues to protest his innocence to this day. Here, Piers Morgan sets out to discover what drove a seemingly well-adjusted family man to kill(n)

programme.desc: Action from the latest edition of the weekly AEW event, featuring all of the biggest stars on the roster, including Adam Page, Chris Jericho, Cody and Jon Moxley(n)

programme.title: James Martin's French Adventure
programme.desc: The chef's culinary tour of France leads him to the diverse and distinct region of the Camargue, a natural region located south of Arles. James begins at Les Marquises, the Laurent family's farm, where he learns about the Camargue bull, and barbecues local fish for the family. Later, he visits a rice farm and cooks a risotto using his own rice-brewed beer(n)

programme.desc: Dr Ranj Singh offers more advice on how to spot, treat and deal with some of the most common illnesses and accidents. As well as going over the latest medical news, he introduces viewers to some of the delicate young lives who are currently in the Paediatric Intensive Care Unit at the Evelina Children's Hospital. Plus, Kylie Pentelow goes to Moorfields Eye Hospital to find out about a breakthrough treatment for glaucoma(n)

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh and the team create a cleverly designed family garden that's full of fun for pre-school tots without being a primary-coloured plastic eyesore for the grownups who share it. Alan, David Domoney, Katie Rushworth and Frances Tophill also give tips on creating a mini-maze, creative sandpits and how to use plants to give an adventurous exotic flavour to play areas(n)

programme.title: Joanna Lumley's Hidden Caribbean: Havana to Haiti
programme.desc: Part one of two. The actress travels across two of the most enigmatic countries in the Caribbean - Cuba and Haiti - to explore, uncover and share the hidden gems they have to offer. Joanna kicks off her adventure in a Havana boxing gym with one of Cuba's rising female boxing stars. She visits the stars of the cabaret at the National Hotel that pre-dates the revolution, and entertains the cigar workers while they roll cigars in their factory. She visits the small town of Hershey, built on the success of the sugar industry, and visits the tomb of Che Guevara before heading off to Hemingway's favourite beach(n)

She visits the stars of the cabaret at the National Hotel that pre-dates the revolution, and entertains the cigar workers while they roll cigars in their factory. She visits the small town of Hershey, built on the success of the sugar industry, and visits the tomb of Che Guevara before heading off to Hemingway's favourite beach(n)

programme.desc: As England and Argentina kick off in the World Cup quarter-final, Oxford's streets are deserted. At an almost empty museum, Adrian Weiss, a specialist in heraldry and genealogy, is found dead - slain by a ceremonial dagger with links to 19th-century India. An entry in the visitors' book leads Morse to a girls' boarding school, where a note reading 'Save Me' is mysteriously slipped into his coat pocket. Digging into the building's troubled history, he finds a connection between Weiss's untimely fate and a series of unsolved murders that occurred on the school's grounds almost 100 years earlier. 1960s-set drama, starring Shaun Evans, with Roger Allam, Anton Lesser, James Bradshaw and Abigail Thaw(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A young woman asks Poirot to investigate her father's murder - a 14-year-old crime for which her mother was found guilty and executed. programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: A gangland killing takes a surprising twist when it turns out the victim has been sexually mutilated. Andy punishes Janet by keeping her out of the heart of the investigation, and when she rebels, the ensuing row becomes personal. Rachel feels ambushed by Sean, who announces his mother wants to meet her - especially as she has spent years trying not to think about her own mum. Suranne Jones, Lesley Sharp, Sean Maguire and Nicholas Gleaves star(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: The surly detective tries to get out of attending a reunion of George Cross holders because a serial rapist has struck again on his patch. Righteous indignation prompts him to take the most desperate professional gamble of his entire career by using PC Hazel Wallace as bait in a trap. He eventually picks up a lead involving the most recent victim's father, but comes under pressure from within the force to back down or be thrown off the case. David Jason stars(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A Midsomer Malham resident is accused of battering her best friend to death, but Barnaby cannot help feeling the evidence against her does not add up. The detective enlists the help of a pensioner, who also happens to be one of the key witnesses, to find out what really happened on the night of the murder - and realises the elderly lady's life could be at risk. With John Nettles, Jason Hughes and Thelma Barlow(n)

programme.desc: The Belgian detective is summoned to a hotel by a mysterious veiled lady who tells him that her forthcoming marriage is under threat because she is being blackmailed over a love letter. Drama, starring David Suchet and Frances Barber(n)

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: As England and Argentina kick off in the World Cup quarter-final, Oxford's streets are deserted. At an almost empty museum, Adrian Weiss, a specialist in heraldry and genealogy, is found dead - slain by a ceremonial dagger with links to 19th-century India. An entry in the visitors' book leads Morse to a girls' boarding school, where a note reading 'Save Me' is mysteriously slipped into his coat pocket. Digging into the building's troubled history, he finds a connection between Weiss's untimely fate and a series of unsolved murders that occurred on the school's grounds almost 100 years earlier. 1960s-set drama, starring Shaun Evans, with Roger Allam, Anton Lesser, James Bradshaw and Abigail Thaw(n)

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Janet comes face to face with the man who left her fighting for life just months earlier - serial killer Geoff Hastings, who has vowed to finally reveal the full extent of his crimes. Rachel and the rest of the team investigate the racially motivated murder of a taxi driver. On the personal front, Andy refuses to take no for an answer, so Janet threatens to report him to the DCI. Crime drama, starring Lesley Sharp and Suranne Jones, with an appearance by Kevin Doyle (Downton Abbey's Molesley)(n)

programme.desc: When wealthy farmer Martin Strickland is discovered bound to a tree, doused in truffle oil and mauled to death by a wild boar, the investigation leads DCI Barnaby to tyrannical celebrity chef Ruth Cameron at the upmarket Wyvern House restaurant. A woman in a sexy cop's uniform turns up dead, and the DCI assumes the killer was her lover - the question is, which one of her many lovers was it? programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: The suspicious death of a Holocaust survivor presents Quincy with a fresh mystery to solve, and sets him on the trail of a Nazi war criminal determined to evade capture(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: A gypsy tries to thwart Theo's pursuit of the serial killer who murdered several members of her clan, as she intends to carry out her own lethal brand of vengeance(n)

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Simon Templar is suspected of involvement in a series of jewel robberies on the French Riviera, and has to stay one step ahead of the police long enough to catch the real culprit. Guest starring Ronnie Barker and Aimee Delamain(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Terry gives shelter to a friend who has broken out of prison, but his act of charity backfires, costing poor old Arthur dearly. Derek Thompson (Casualty) and Sherrie Hewson (Loose Women) feature alongside George Cole and Dennis Waterman(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie and Doyle are assigned to protect a deposed African politician, who is due to attend a conference with a view to regaining power. After foiling two assassination attempts, they soon appreciate others share information on his whereabouts. Charles Dance and Geoffrey Palmer feature alongside stars Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: The Flying Squad foils the hijacking of a security truck before attempting to learn the identity of an informer. Colin Welland guest stars in the hard-hitting police drama, alongside John Thaw and Dennis Waterman(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: The medical examiner comes to the aid of an agoraphobic woman when she claims to have witnessed a murder, even though there is no sign of a body(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: A woman is the only witness to the murder of a police officer, but her gangster boyfriend is an old enemy of Theo, and tries to intimidate her into keeping quiet(n)

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Simon Templar encounters a girl who claims to be Hitler's daughter, and helps her evade Neo-Nazis. Vintage adventure, starring Roger Moore(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur sees an opportunity to cash in when a footballer with a drink and gambling habit goes missing, but selling his story to the paper is not as easy as it seems when there is more than one person interested in his whereabouts. Comedy drama, with George Cole and Dennis Waterman(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie and Doyle suspect Russian intervention when a mentally unstable lieutenant colonel holding valuable Nato secrets disappears from an army psychiatric hospital. Lewis Collins, Martin Shaw and Gordon Jackson star(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: With Haskins in Toronto, his stand-in DCI Quirk (guest star Bill Maynard) becomes obsessed with catching a gang of thieves. When information is received suggesting the crooks are about to rob a jeweller's shop, officers are poised for action, but events take an unexpected turn as the informant seemingly has a score to settle with Quirk first. Hard-hitting police drama, with John Thaw and Dennis Waterman(n)

programme.title: Cycling: Paris-Nice Highlights
programme.desc: Gary Imlach presents action from stage two, featuring a 166.5km course from Chevreuse to Chalette-sur-Loing. With commentary by Ned Boulting and David Millar, and reports from Daniel Friebe(n)

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: On a volcanic island in the Pacific Ring of Fire, an ancient fishing community is being terrorised by something in the water that has left behind a trail of bloodied victims. With a collective knowledge built up over centuries, these fishermen thought they knew everything in these waters - until now. The only clue they have to the attacker's identity are the large puncture wounds it has left behind. Here, angling detective Jeremy Wade journeys to this land of fire and folklore to put himself to the ultimate test - can he uncover the identity of this mysterious fanged assailant?(n)

programme.title: Giant Lobster Hunters
programme.desc: With the winter lobster season in full swing, Squizzy, the skipper of Bold Contender, is under pressure to fill a special order of 600 brindle lobsters. The engine of Ansons Bay stops working, and stormy weather and rough seas plague the Minnamurra, while Danny, the captain of William Norling, receives some good news when hunting for big reds in the Bass Strait(n)

programme.title: Magnum Force (1973)
programme.desc: Maverick cop Dirty Harry Callahan antagonises his superiors once more in his pursuit of a vigilante group responsible for murdering the city's most wanted criminals. However, he is unaware that the killers are some of his fellow officers - and are prepared to see him dead to keep their secret safe. Action thriller sequel, starring Clint Eastwood, Hal Holbrook, Mitchell Ryan, David Soul and Robert Urich(n)

programme.desc: On a volcanic island in the Pacific Ring of Fire, an ancient fishing community is being terrorised by something in the water that has left behind a trail of bloodied victims. With a collective knowledge built up over centuries, these fishermen thought they knew everything in these waters - until now. The only clue they have to the attacker's identity are the large puncture wounds it has left behind. Here, angling detective Jeremy Wade journeys to this land of fire and folklore to put himself to the ultimate test - can he uncover the identity of this mysterious fanged assailant?(n)

Edward offers to help Wills win his Duke of Edinburgh Gold Award, but they end up being kidnapped by a farmer who uses them as cheap labour. When Victory was built more than 40 years previously, she was the largest warship in the world with firepower equivalent to a whole army on land. However, Rob discovers that this titan of the seas was very nearly scrapped long before the battle that would ultimately seal her place in history(n)

programme.title: Elizabeth I and II: Britain's Golden Queens
programme.desc: Historical documentary examining whether there are similarities between the two monarchs, from their ascensions to the throne to the battles they faced to protect their reigns. In the first episode, Elizabeth II is born a minor princess in the second rank of the royal family and grows up away from the limelight. Elizabeth I is the only child of Henry VIII and his second wife Anne Boleyn but, as a girl, she is an immediate disappointment. After Henry executes her mother, the young Elizabeth is cast into the wilderness. However, for both Elizabeths an extraordinary sequence of events will catapult them both onto the throne and their journey through trauma and disruption will make them strong(n)

programme.title: The Royal Family at War
programme.desc: Part two of two. Biographer Penny Junor recalls the rumoured rift between the Queen and the Duchess of Cornwall, Princess Margaret's relationship with divorcee Peter Townsend is assessed, and Prince Philip's falling out with the former Duchess of York is revealed(n)

programme.title: Shannon Matthews: The Mother's Story
programme.desc: The disappearance of nine-year-old Shannon Matthews in 2008 gripped the nation. Her mother Karen claimed that her daughter had gone missing on her way home from school, prompting a nationwide search. But weeks later, it was revealed that Karen had known the truth all along - Shannon was being kept hidden by a man known to her mother, who had plotted to claim the £50,000 reward money for her return. This documentary tries to get at the truth of a woman many have branded as evil, asking what drove her to do it, how she can rebuild her life and whether she is sorry for her actions(n)

programme.title: When Kids Kill
programme.desc: Trainee accountant Stuart Harling was obsessed with an ultraviolent video game, and in 2006 killed nurse Cheryl Moss by stabbing her 72 times. A-level student Steven Miles watched nothing but crime drama Dexter, and in 2014 dismembered his girlfriend. Aaron Roach Bridgeman talks with experts associated with the cases, contemplating whether any connection can be proven between entertainment and actions(n)

programme.title: The 21.co.uk Live Casino Show
programme.desc: Interactive gambling(n)

programme.title: Our Dream Hotel
programme.desc: Alex Polizzi comes to the aid of Jason and Sirem, who left Southampton for a new life running a guest house in Turkey, building their business from scratch into a functioning hotel(n)

programme.title: Tribal Teens - Here Comes Trouble
programme.desc: Nineteen-year-old nightclub dancer Courtney and 17-year-old ultra-vain Charlie face up to life on the frozen Mongolian plains as they spend a week with a family of hardy Kazakhs in a communal tent. The pair consume the local cuisine - including horse meat - and contend with only the most basic sanitation. They also learn to appreciate that for the Kazakh tribe, even getting water involves a major expedition to a frozen river to hack out blocks of ice(n)

programme.title: House Doctor
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton presents as interior design expert Ann Maurice casts her eye over a cluttered home in Henley-on-Thames, Oxfordshire. Unfinished paintwork and dated details are among the factors giving the property a 'youth hostel' feel, which despite the asking price being lowered three times is still on the market after six months(n)

programme.title: Great Scientists
programme.desc: Profile of 16th-century Italian astronomer Galileo Galilei, whose observations through a telescope of his own invention led him to defend the Copernican thesis that Earth orbited the sun instead of vice versa - a view that got him into trouble with the Inquisition. Presented by Allan Chapman(n)

programme.title: Wildlife SOS
programme.desc: The work of volunteers treating sick, injured and orphaned animals at a wildlife sanctuary(n)

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa and her family go to Italy, where Daddy Pig has trouble driving on the other side of the road(n)

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: The piglet and her family spend the first day in their Italian holiday villa before visiting a nearby village, where they find plenty of things to buy(n)

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: The piglet and her family return from their holiday, while Suzy has missed her friend and is eager to see her again(n)

programme.title: Peppa Pig

programme.desc: Round-up of the day's headlines from around the world(n)

programme.desc: Showbiz news and gossip(n)
